originalTitle: On Trails is a wandering tale that blends hiking, science, and history
summary: Hiking is one of life's great joys. Turning off the screens and stepping out into nature for an extended period of time, perhaps even several days, is rejuvenating. Unfortunately, as someone with two young kids and a bad back, I'm not really able to go backpacking anymore. So I often find myself trying to live […]
content: Hiking is one of life’s great joys. Turning off the screens and stepping out into nature for an extended period of time, perhaps even several days, is rejuvenating. Unfortunately, as someone with two young kids and a bad back, I’m not really able to go backpacking anymore. So I often find myself trying to live vicariously through others who write about their lengthy travails along the Appalachian or the PCT. That’s what I thought I was signing up for when I picked up On Trails: An Exploration by Robert Moor. But it turned out to be so much more. The prologue starts with Moor talking about his decision to thru-hike the Appalachian Trail. And chapter one doesn’t stray too far from the expected subject matter either. It focuses primarily on Moor’s trip to Western Brook Pond in Newfoundland and broadly discusses the concept of wilderness.  His talents as a writer are apparent from moment one. A storm pins Moor down on a ridge: > For the better part of an hour, awash in mounting waves of tympanic rumble, I had time to reconsider the merits of hiking. Stripped of its Romantic finery, the wild ceased to inspire; only a gauzy scrim separated sublimity and horror. This is perhaps the first hint that what you’re in for is not some travelogue or a simple memoir that uses the trail as a narrative device. Chapter two immediately solidifies this, launching a discussion of ant trails and the fine distinctions of various English words for lines of movement. On Trails bounces around gleefully from topic to topic: Game trails, fiber optic wires, Moor’s stint as a shepherd. And all throughout, Moor seamlessly navigates shifting tones. One moment, he’s waxing poetic about the power of nature, the next, he’s spinning an anecdote about misplacing an entire flock of sheep with a comic’s sense of pacing, then turning philosophical about the damage done by colonialism. It’s a testament to Moor’s skill that the book not only manages to be compulsively readable, but never feels disjointed as he swings wildly from exploring a proto-internet envisioned by engineer Vannevar Bush in 1945, to quoting poet Gary Snyder. On Trails starts with a simple idea: how did the Appalachian Trail, or any hiking trail for that matter, form? And from there it branches off endlessly into a thousand different tributaries, exploring how the very concept of trails can help us understand the world. On Trails is available on most ebook stores, but I highly recommend you buy a physical copy at your local independent bookshop or support your local library.
chineseTitle: On Trails 是一个融合了徒步旅行、科学和历史的流浪故事
chineseSummary: 徒步旅行是人生一大乐趣。关掉屏幕，长时间（甚至可能是几天）走进大自然，可以让人恢复活力。不幸的是，作为一个有两个孩子和背部不好的人，我真的不能再去背包旅行了。所以我经常发现自己试图生活[...]
chineseContent: 徒步旅行是人生一大乐趣。关掉屏幕，长时间（甚至可能是几天）走进大自然，可以让人恢复活力。不幸的是，作为一个有两个孩子和背部不好的人，我真的不能再去背包旅行了。因此，我经常发现自己试图通过其他人来代替生活，他们写下了他们沿着阿巴拉契亚山脉或 PCT 的漫长艰辛。当我拿起罗伯特·摩尔的《小径：一次探索》时，我以为我要报名的就是这个。但事实证明，事情远不止这些。序言从摩尔谈论他徒步穿越阿巴拉契亚小道的决定开始。第一章也没有偏离预期的主题太远。它主要关注摩尔前往纽芬兰西布鲁克池塘的旅行，并广泛讨论了荒野的概念。  他作为作家的才华从一开始就显而易见。一场暴风雨将摩尔镇压在了山脊上： > 在一个多小时的时间里，我被鼓膜隆隆声淹没，有时间重新考虑徒步旅行的好处。失去了浪漫的华美，狂野不再激发灵感。只有一层薄纱将崇高与恐怖分开。这也许是第一个暗示，表明你所读的不是一些游记或一本使用路径作为叙事手段的简单回忆录。第二章立即巩固了这一点，开始讨论蚂蚁的踪迹以及各种英语单词的运动路线的细微差别。 《小径上》兴高采烈地从一个话题跳到另一个话题：游戏小径、光纤线路、摩尔作为牧羊人的经历。在整个过程中，摩尔无缝地驾驭着不断变化的基调。一会儿，他对自然的力量充满诗意，一会儿，他就编造了一个轶事，讲述了漫画中的羊群被放错地方的故事。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
